rulings.Case Title: JANE RONGERUDE VS. LANNIE JEW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jennifer Jew'S Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens Or In The Alternative To Require Claimant To Provide Undertaking And For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 30, 2007, LINE 22. DEFENDANT JENNIFER JEW'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO REQUIRE CLAIMANT TO PROVIDE UNDERTAKING AND FOR ATTORNEY FEES: HEARING REQUIRED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM INVOLVES AN INTEREST IN PROPERTY, IT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS CAN BE SATISFIED BY MONEY DAMAGES. BY WITHHOLDING THE RENT, PLAINTIFF IS TAKING STEPS TO PROTECT THEIR CLAIM FOR DAMAGES BASED ON LACK OF HABITABLE CONDITIONS. THERE APPEARS TO BE NO DISPUTE AS TO PLAINTIFF'S LEASEHOLD INTEREST AND THE COURT IS NOT AWARE OF AUTHORITY TO GRANT PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO "PROHIBIT AN ILLEGAL EVICTION". THE PURPOSE OF THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER STATUTE IS TO PROTECT AGAINST SUCH AN EVENT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LUXIM CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. JONES DAY, AN OHIO PARTNERSHIP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against Jones Day
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 30, 2007, LINE 15. PLAINTIFF LUXIM CORPORATION'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against Jones Day: The motion for a preliminary injunction is denied. The authority of the Court over the discipline of counsel is not preempted by the Patent and Trademark Office ("PTO") rules and regulations. However, as a matter of comity, it is not appropriate for one court to usurp the responsibility of another court to control the conduct of counsel who are appearing before the other court. In Re Complex Asbestos Litigation (1991) 232 Cal.App.3d 572, 600-601. Here, the dispute at issue is centered in the PTO and the United Kingdom; neither matter involves the practice of law in this court or, for that matter, in any of the courts of California. The fact that counsel may be in California is not a sufficient reason for this court to usurp the responsibility and judgment of the jurists, who actually are involved in the cases at issue. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 1061, the Court declines to exercise its authority to decide this case. Plaintiff has a readily available remedy to address plaintiff's concerns by requesting a ruling from the PTO and the applicable court in the United Kingdom. No testimony will be taken at the hearing. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KGO TELEVISION INC VS. MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Clarify The Scope Of The Court'S April 12,07, Order And/Or Modify That Order
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 30, 2007, LINE 17. PETITIONER K.G.O. TELEVISION, INC.'S Motion To Clarify The Scope Of The Court'S April 12, 2007, Order And/Or Modify That Order IS DENIED. THE ENTIRE FOCUS OF THE WRIT WAS ON THE DISCLOSURE OF THE NAMES OF CERTAIN MUNI DRIVERS WHO RECEIVED PASSENGER SERVICE REPORTS. THE COURT RULED THAT THE NAMES OF THE DRIVERS WERE NOT TO BE DISCLOSED. THE WRIT DID NOT ADDRESS VIDEOTAPES. TO THE EXTENT THAT VIDEOTAPES DISCLOSE THE IDENTITY OF THE DRIVERS, THE PRIOR ORDER PRECLUDES THE DISCLOSURE. NO EVIDENCE WAS PRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT ANY OF THE IDENTITIES OF ANY OF THE DRIVERS WERE "KNOWN". IF K.G.O. WISHES TO AMEND THE WRIT, THESE ISSUES CAN BE ADDRESSED IN A NOTICED HEARING. =(302/PJM/AP)
